question: How many PTO days do I get after 3 years?
answer: According to the current PTO and Leave Policy (HR-001), for employees with 2-5 years of tenure, you accrue 24 days of PTO annually, which translates to a monthly accrual rate of 2.0 days/month (PTO and Leave Policy, HR-001).
contexts:
Tenure | Annual PTO Days | Accrual Rate
0-2 years | 20 days | 1.67 days/month
2-5 years | 24 days | 2.0 days/month
5+ years | 28 days | 2.33 days/month

Tenure | Annual PTO Days | Accrual Rate
0-2 years | 15 days | 1.25 days/month
2-5 years | 18 days | 1.5 days/month
5+ years | 22 days | 1.83 days/month
Submit PTO requests through your manager via email at least 10 business days in advance.
Employees may carry over up to 5 unused PTO days into the next calendar year. Unused days beyond that are forfeited on December 31st.
Submit PTO requests through the HR portal at least 5 business days in advance for planned leave. Manager approval is required for absences longer than 5 consecutive days.
